model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Illinois is the safer pick because the Illini own a clear full-season predictive edge, ranking No. 6 in Torvik and No. 7 in KenPom versus North Carolina around No. 27 to 29, with a massive offensive advantage driven by one of the nation’s best attacks. North Carolina has a credible path if it slows the game and wins a more balanced half-court battle, but Illinois’ scoring ceiling and stronger overall efficiency profile make it the more reliable choice to advance. I did not find a confirmed availability issue significant enough to change that outlook.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois over North Carolina on superior efficiency ceiling
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois rates clearly higher in the major predictive systems and brings an elite offense that separates it from a good but less explosive North Carolina team. UNC can absolutely make this competitive with its solid defense, but without a meaningful health edge or stronger underlying profile, the Tar Heels have the narrower path.
evidence: Illinois: Torvik No. 6 and KenPom No. 7.
Illinois offense: Torvik adjusted offense 131.96 (No. 2), KenPom adjusted offense 131.2 (No. 1).
North Carolina: Torvik No. 27 and KenPom No. 29.
North Carolina offense/defense: Torvik adjusted offense 121.52 (No. 32), adjusted defense 100.05 (No. 34); KenPom adjusted offense 121.5 (No. 33), adjusted defense 100.6.
Both teams are 24-8, so record does not erase Illinois’ much larger efficiency margin.
News searches did not surface a confirmed major injury or availability issue that materially changes the matchup.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
